title: Perineuronal Net Mapping and Quantification with Quint
author: Joseph Martin
materials: Continue until all images are mapped Video 2.
low intensity, moderately-defined PNNs (moderate green) Label
diffuse PNN staining (dim green signal) Label

steps.title: Original Quint documentation
steps.instruction: Original Quint documentation: https://quint-workflow.readthedocs.io/en/latest/QUINTintro.html Other protocols using Quint include: https://www.protocols.io/view/regional-mouse-brain-analysis-modified-quint-kqdg3xbkzg25/v1 Videos of selected steps (also linked individually throughout the protocol): https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLQCli8HoaKipZD4ZB95tL_6uU-TQ4X3_G&si=UMTu8uUnU0Kypumb

steps.title: Initial image prep (PowerRename, Nutil, FileBuilder)...
steps.instruction: Initial image prep (PowerRename, Nutil, FileBuilder) Mapping (QuickNII) Non-linear transformation (VisuAlign) Pixel Classification (Ilastik) Object Classification (Ilastik, Fiji) Quantification (Nutil, Scripts) 3D Visualization (MeshView, optional)

steps.title: Create a "Quint" folder on your...
steps.instruction: Create a "Quint" folder on your C drive for all software installation

steps.title: Download all software from the following links
steps.instruction: Download all software from the following links: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/powertoys/install (We used PowerToysUserSetup-0.85.1-x

steps.title: https://www
steps.instruction: https://www.ilastik.org/documentation/basics/installation (We used version 1.2.0.post1-gpu) Make sure to install the appropriate QuickNII atlas version for your images Mice: We used ABAMouse-v3-2017 Rats: We used WHSRat-v4

steps.title: Unzip all downloads within the "Quint" folder that you created
steps.instruction: Unzip all downloads within the "Quint" folder that you created

steps.title: If desired, create desktop shortcuts for...
steps.instruction: If desired, create desktop shortcuts for each software program or pin them to the task bar

steps.title: Open all software programs to ensure that they are functional
steps.instruction: Open all software programs to ensure that they are functional

steps.title: Download and duplicate a copy of...
steps.instruction: Download and duplicate a copy of the "QUINT Template Folders found here: QUINT Template Folders-20260205T120135Z-1-001.zip Note: for sagittal sections, you will need both "Left" and "Right" folders that each contain the Quint template folders. They will essentially be treated as separate brains for the purpose of Quint.

steps.title: Obtain images (Likely TIFFs) Example of...
steps.instruction: Obtain images (Likely TIFFs) Example of file nomenclature at this point: #111722_NW_PNN_1.1.1.tif

steps.title: Put images in “A. Original TIFFs” Note
steps.instruction: Put images in “A. Original TIFFs” Note: If images were excluded for quality control purposes, the images that you should map will be in afolder called “AA. Original TIFFs, Images Excluded”

steps.title: Ensure that images are ordered and...
steps.instruction: Ensure that images are ordered and in the correct orientation Coronal: ordered rostral to caudal, dorsal on the top, left on the left Sagittal: ordered lateral to medial, dorsal on the top, rostral on the left Example of file nomenclature at this point: 01#111722_NW_PNN_1.1.1.tif

steps.title: Copy images from A
steps.instruction: Copy images from A. (or AA.) and paste them into "B. Renamed TIFFs"

steps.title: Rename images to ensure they are...
steps.instruction: Rename images to ensure they are compatible with Quint software Select all images and right-click Select "Rename with PowerRename" Use PowerRename to replace all periods, hyphens, and hashtags in the file name with an underscore (uses a "find and replace"-based interface" Note: ensure that "apply to" is set to "Filename only" to avoid corrupting files 4. Add a serialized number in the form "_sXXX" to the end of each file name (example: _s001 for the first image) – possible with PowerRename Change "apply to" to "Filename + extension" Type ".tif" in the top box and "_s${padding=3,start=1}.tif in the bottom box Click apply once Example of file nomenclature at this point: 01_111722_NW_PNN_1_1_1_s001.png Video 1: PowerRename Workflow

steps.title: Resize images (downsizing so they will...
steps.instruction: Resize images (downsizing so they will be compatible with Quint requirements) Open Nutil Select "Operation" – "New" – "Resize" Name the project according to the cohort/condition you are working on Use "B. Renamed TIFFs" as the Input Folder Use "C. PNGs (with section numbers)" as the Output Folder Make sure "Percent" is selected as the Resize Type Set the Resize Factor to 85 (coronal) or 60 (sagittal) Select "Save As," name the resize file, then save it to "C. PNGs (with section numbers)" Click "Start" Open FileBuilder (located in the QuickNII download folder) Navigate to "C. PNGs (with section numbers)" within your copy of the Quint template folders, select all images (CTRL + A), and click "Open" Ensure that all remarks in the rightmost column are either green or orange (NOT red) If any remarks are red, repeat steps 1-12, decreasing the Resize Factor by 10 (75 for coronal or 50 for sagittal) Repeat steps 1-13 as needed until all remarks are either green or orange 13. Select "Save XML" name the file according to the cohort/condition, and save it to "D. QuickNII" 14. Copy and paste all PNGs from "C." to "D." – if your images don't show up in QuickNII, you probably forgot to do this

steps.title: Opening QuickNII Open QuickNII (use appropriate...
steps.instruction: Opening QuickNII Open QuickNII (use appropriate version for species (mouse/rat) ) Select "Manage Data" (top right) Open the mapping window (first window that pops up when you open QuickNII) on your left monitor, and move the data management window (which pops up when you click "Manage Data") to your right monitor In the window that pops up, select "Load" (bottom left) Select and open the XML file from your "D." folder In the data management window, double click on an image's filename to prepare to map it. This should make it appear in the mapping window. If an image does not appear, make sure that images are present in the "D. QuickNII" folder. This is a common mistake. Click on "Values and Control" on the bottom left of the mapping window. If this isn't visible, try hovering your mouse over the bottom left of the mapping window, and an option to drag a menu up from the bottom should appear.

steps.title: If your images are generally dark,...
steps.instruction: If your images are generally dark, you can use the "Image Darkness" slider to brighten the image

steps.title: Mouse only
steps.instruction: Mouse only: To see the outlines of regions, select "Outline" and change your atlas type to either "Rainbow 2017" or "Original 2017"

steps.title: Sagittal only
steps.instruction: Sagittal only: Use the bottommost window on the right side of the screen to change lateral/medial positioning. Ensure that left hemisphere tissues are mapped on the left side of the atlas and right hemisphere tissues are mapped on the right side of the atlas.

steps.title: Sagittal only
steps.instruction: Sagittal only: Change the angles in the red (coronal), green (horizontal), and blue (sagittal) boxes to either 180 or 0 in order to orient your tissues correctly (dorsal on the top, rostral on the left)

steps.title: Start mapping by choosing approximately 3...
steps.instruction: Start mapping by choosing approximately 3 images with clear landmarks to map initially (ideally equally distributed across the rostrocaudal/mediolateral axis)

steps.title: Map the image (see subsequent steps for Mapping Mechanics)
steps.instruction: Map the image (see subsequent steps for Mapping Mechanics)

steps.title: Select "Store" (top left) to save...
steps.instruction: Select "Store" (top left) to save your mapped image and apply changes to remaining unmapped images.

steps.title: Mapping Landmarks (Coronal) Anterior Commissure Lateral...
steps.instruction: Mapping Landmarks (Coronal) Anterior Commissure Lateral Ventricles Hippocampus Mapping Landmarks (Sagittal) Hippocampus Piriform cortex

steps.title: Choose an viewing mode in the...
steps.instruction: Choose an viewing mode in the top left corner of the mapping window. For mice, STPt avg 2015 works well for initial alignment, and Rainbow 2017 (with "Outline" checked in the Values and Controls settings) works well for refinement. For rats, MRI works well for initial alignment, and Segmentation (with "Outline" checked in the Values and Controls settings) works well for refinement.

steps.title: Adjust the opacity of the atlas...
steps.instruction: Adjust the opacity of the atlas using the slider on the left side of the mapping window. Pressing "H" (button on screen or with keyboard) displays only your image, while pressing "V" (button on screen or with keyboard) displays only the atlas

steps.title: To adjust mediolateral position , drag...
steps.instruction: To adjust mediolateral position , drag the red dot in the Horizontal View (bottom right panel) from left to right Video 2.

steps.title: To carry out rostrocaudal linear translation...
steps.instruction: To carry out rostrocaudal linear translation , click and drag the "image" containing the atlas left and right across the main mapping window Video 2.

steps.title: To carry out rostrocaudal scaling (stretching)...
steps.instruction: To carry out rostrocaudal scaling (stretching) , press the spacebar while holding the mouse pointer over the place you want the reference point for scaling. A small cross will appear. Usually it is easier to choose a side and not place the cross in the middle of the section. Then, click on the scaling button (top left, double-headed left-right arrow) and a drouble arrow will appear. Place your mouse pointer at the opposite side of the double cross and press the left button of your mouse. While keeping the left button of the mouse pressed, you can now gently drag the atlas in the direction indicated by the double arrow. Video 2.

steps.title: To carry out dorsoventral linear translation...
steps.instruction: To carry out dorsoventral linear translation , click and drag the "image" containing the atlas up and down in the main mapping window. Video 2.

steps.title: To carry out dorsoventral scaling ,...
steps.instruction: To carry out dorsoventral scaling , press the spacebar while holding the mouse pointer over the place you want the reference point for scaling. A small cross will appear. Usually it is easier to choose a side and not place the cross in the middle of the section. Then, click on the scaling button (top left, double-headed up-down arrow): a double arrow will appear. Place your mouse pointer at the opposite side of the double cross, and press the left button of your mouse. While keeping the left button of the mouse pressed, you can now gently drag the atlas in the direction indicated by the double arrow. Video 2.

steps.title: To carry out rotation , use...
steps.instruction: To carry out rotation , use the small, curved arrows (upper left) to rotate the atlas in relation to your image. Video 2.

steps.title: To carry out mediolateral angle change...
steps.instruction: To carry out mediolateral angle change (addressing sectioning asymmetry), click and drag the yellow line on the Coronal View (right panel) up and down Video 2.

steps.title: To carry out dorsoventral angle change...
steps.instruction: To carry out dorsoventral angle change (addressing sectioning asymmetry), click and drag the yellow line on the Horizontal View (right panel) left and right Video 2.

steps.title: Choose an viewing mode in the...
steps.instruction: Choose an viewing mode in the top left corner of the mapping window. For mice, STPt avg 2015 works well for initial alignment, and Rainbow 2017 (with "Outline" checked in the Values and Controls settings) works well for refinement. For rats, MRI works well for initial alignment, and Segmentation (with "Outline" checked in the Values and Controls settings) works well for refinement.

steps.title: Adjust the opacity of the atlas...
steps.instruction: Adjust the opacity of the atlas using the slider on the left side of the mapping window. Pressing "H" (button on screen or with keyboard) displays only your image, while pressing "V" (button on screen or with keyboard) displays only the atlas

steps.title: To adjust rostrocaudal position , drag...
steps.instruction: To adjust rostrocaudal position , drag the red dot in the Horizontal View (bottom right panel) from left to right Video 2.

steps.title: To carry out mediolateral linear translation...
steps.instruction: To carry out mediolateral linear translation , click and drag the "image" containing the atlas left and right across the main mapping window Video 2.

steps.title: To carry out mediolateral scaling (stretching)...
steps.instruction: To carry out mediolateral scaling (stretching) , press the spacebar while holding the mouse pointer over the place you want the reference point for scaling. A small cross will appear. Usually it is easier to choose a side and not place the cross in the middle of the section. Then, click on the scaling button (top left, double-headed left-right arrow) and a drouble arrow will appear. Place your mouse pointer at the opposite side of the double cross and press the left button of your mouse. While keeping the left button of the mouse pressed, you can now gently drag the atlas in the direction indicated by the double arrow. Video 2.

steps.title: To carry out dorsoventral linear translation...
steps.instruction: To carry out dorsoventral linear translation , click and drag the "image" containing the atlas up and down in the main mapping window. Video 2.

steps.title: To carry out dorsoventral scaling ,...
steps.instruction: To carry out dorsoventral scaling , press the spacebar while holding the mouse pointer over the place you want the reference point for scaling. A small cross will appear. Usually it is easier to choose a side and not place the cross in the middle of the section. Then, click on the scaling button (top left, double-headed up-down arrow): a double arrow will appear. Place your mouse pointer at the opposite side of the double cross, and press the left button of your mouse. While keeping the left button of the mouse pressed, you can now gently drag the atlas in the direction indicated by the double arrow. Video 2.

steps.title: To carry out rotation , use...
steps.instruction: To carry out rotation , use the small, curved arrows (upper left) to rotate the atlas in relation to your image. Video 2.

steps.title: To carry out rostrocaudal angle change...
steps.instruction: To carry out rostrocaudal angle change (addressing sectioning asymmetry), click and drag the yellow line on the Coronal View (right panel) up and down Video 2.

steps.title: To carry out dorsoventral angle change...
steps.instruction: To carry out dorsoventral angle change (addressing sectioning asymmetry), click and drag the yellow line on the Horizontal View (right panel) left and right Video 2.

steps.title: Check the graph on the right...
steps.instruction: Check the graph on the right side of the data management window. If your images are ordered and mapped correctly, the graph should be approximately linear and monotonic (always increasing).

steps.title: Click "Save JSON" (data management window,...
steps.instruction: Click "Save JSON" (data management window, bottom left), name the file, and save it to "D. QuickNII" Note: If you need to save your progress in QuickNII for any reason, use this step and name the file logically according to your progress

steps.title: Preparation Paste all images from "C
steps.instruction: Preparation Paste all images from "C. PNGs with Section Numbers" to your "E. VisuAlign" folder Open VisuAlign Click "File" (top left) – "Open" and open your saved QuickNII file from "D." Drag the opacity slider in the upper left corner all the way to the right so that the atlas appears as an outline Change the marker/outline color(s) as desired so that they are more visible Video 3.1: Opening VisuAlign

steps.title: Adding Markers and Making Adjustments Hover...
steps.instruction: Adding Markers and Making Adjustments Hover the mouse over a specific part of an image Press the spacebar to add a marker (looks like a plus sign) Click and drag the marker in the direction that adjustment needs to be made Note: Avoid making more than ~50 annotations per image Video 3.2: Adding VisuAlign Markers Deleting Markers Hover the mouse over the marker to be deleted Press the delete key

steps.title: Steps for Each Image
steps.instruction: Steps for Each Image: Add markers to areas of the tissue that are already mapped and looked good (usually toward the middle of the image or at landmarks). Do not make any adjustments to these markers. (Adding markers here just "locks" things into place and keeps you from "over-adjusting). Begin adding markers to areas of the tissue that need adjustment. Generally begin with the tissue outline before working your way inward. Ensure that key landmarks (hippocampus, ventricles, differentiation of cortex and corpus callosum, cortical layers) Use the single arrows (top right) to navigate between images. Other arrow options allow you to move forward to backward 10 images or to navigate to the beginning or end images Saving Progress: After carrying out nonlinear transformation for all images, select "File" – "Save As", name the file, and save it to "E. VisuAlign" Note: If you need to save your progress in VisuAlign for any reason, use this step and name the file logically. Select "File" – "Export". Set the save location to "E. VisuAlign" Copy and paste all files within "E. VisuAlign" with names ending in ".flat" to the "Atlas_Maps" folder within "I. Nutil" Note: If you are trying to open and reload a saved VisuAlign state, make sure that your images (PNGs) are in the same folder that your VisuAlign file is saved to. (Otherwise, nothing will show up when you open the file).

steps.title: Copy and paste all images (in PNG format) from "C
steps.instruction: Copy and paste all images (in PNG format) from "C. PNGs with Section Numbers" to the folder "A. Images", which is located within "G. Ilastik"

steps.title: Under "Create New Project", choose "Pixel Classification"
steps.instruction: Under "Create New Project", choose "Pixel Classification". Name and save the project file within "G. Ilastik"

steps.title: Upload several training images that are...
steps.instruction: Upload several training images that are representative of the dataset you are analyzing (Usually at least 1 image per brain/condition)

steps.title: For the first training image, double-click...
steps.instruction: For the first training image, double-click the box in the column "Location" for each image

steps.title: In the popup window, next to...
steps.instruction: In the popup window, next to the heading "Storage", choose the option to "Copy into project file"

steps.title: Click "Ok"
steps.instruction: Click "Ok"

steps.title: Repeat for all training images
steps.instruction: Repeat for all training images

steps.title: Click and drag over all white boxes
steps.instruction: Click and drag over all white boxes. They should all turn green with check marks.

steps.title: Click "Add Label" twice (should be 4 labels now
steps.instruction: Click "Add Label" twice (should be 4 labels now: Label 1, Label 2, Label 3 Label

steps.title: Double-click on each label and change...
steps.instruction: Double-click on each label and change both the "Color for drawing" and "Color for probability display" to the following for each of the respective labels: Label 1 (high-intensity PNNs): bright green Label 2 (moderate-intensity PNNs) : dark green Label 3 (diffuse PNN signal): bright blue Label 4 (background): dark blue

steps.title: Determining which PNNS are high-intensity Open...
steps.instruction: Determining which PNNS are high-intensity Open your training images in ImageJ Select "Image" – "Adjust" – "Brightness/Contrast" Turn contrast all the way up Only label PNNs that are clearly-defined in this view as high-intensity. All others are low intensity

steps.title: Batch Processing Select "4
steps.instruction: Batch Processing Select "4. Prediction Export" Set "Source:" to "Probabilities" Click "Choose Export Image Settings" In the window the pops up, ensure that within the "Output File Info" section, the format is "hdf5" Click "Ok" Select "5. Batch Processing" Click "Select Raw Data Files" Navigate to and select all images in your "A. Images" folder (within "G. Ilastik") Click "Open" Click "Process All Files". This can take several minutes Once processing is finished, cut and paste all simple segmentation files (labeled with "Probabilities.h5", solid white icons) from your "A. Images" folder to your "B. Probabilities (Pixel Classification)" folder

steps.title: high intensity, well-defined PNNs (brightest green,...
steps.instruction: high intensity, well-defined PNNs (brightest green, visible on high-contrast ImageJ image) Label

steps.title: Toggle "Uncertainty" eyeball icon to see...
steps.instruction: Toggle "Uncertainty" eyeball icon to see areas where the model is unsure of segmentation. Focus on these areas (highlighted in blue) to add annotations and refine segmentation Saving segmentation: When satisfied with your segmentation (and at any point), save your Ilastik project by clicking "Project" (top left) – "Save Project"

steps.title: Input Data" (left panel) – "Add New"
steps.instruction: Input Data" (left panel) – "Add New". Upload the same training images you used for Pixel Classification For the first training image, double-click the box in the column "Location" for each image In the popup window, next to the heading "Storage", choose the option to "Copy into project file" Click "Ok" Repeat for all training images Click "Prediction Maps" Select "

steps.title: Input Data"(left panel) – "Add" –...
steps.instruction: Input Data"(left panel) – "Add" – "Add Separate Image(s)" Select all of the probability files corresponding to your training images (from "B. Probabilities (Pixel Classification" Change the storage locations to "Copy into project file" for each probability file you upload Select "

steps.title: Threshold and Size Filter" Note
steps.instruction: Threshold and Size Filter" Note: The thresholds and parameters mentioned in the following steps can be changed based on your project and focus, but make sure to stay consistent Method – "Simple" Input – "0" Smooth – both boxes "0.6" Threshold – "0.5" Size filter – Min: "0" Max "400" – This is the main number you may want to manipulate. In later steps, you can always lower the cutoff for the maximum if desired, but once lowered here, you cannot lower it later, so err on the side of a higher cutoff if unsure. Click "Apply" and make sure everything looks reasonable Select "

steps.title: Object Feature Selection" Click "Select Features
steps.instruction: Object Feature Selection" Click "Select Features: Click the arrow next to "Standard Object Features" Click the checkbox next to "Standard Object Features: Deselect the following three checkboxes under the heading "Location": "Bounding Box Maximum", "Bounding Box Minimum", and "Center of the Object" Click "Ok" (bottom right of object feature selection window) Note: it is not necessary to do anything in the "

steps.title: Object Classification" window, but you can...
steps.instruction: Object Classification" window, but you can experiment with turning on and off the various prediction and segmentation options if you would like. Using the segmentation views, each individual PNN in the "Label 1" category should appear as differently colored blobs

steps.title: Batch Processing Select "5
steps.instruction: Batch Processing Select "5. Object Information Export" Source – "Object Identities" Click "Choose Export Image Settings" Under "Output File Info", set Format – "tiff" Click "Ok" Select "6. Batch Processing" Click "Select Raw Data Files" Navigate to and select all your images in your "A. Images" folder (within "G. Ilastik") Click "Open" Click "Prediction Maps" Navigate to and select the probability files corresponding to all the images that you just selected (from the appropriate "B. Probabilities (Pixel Classification)" folder) Click "Open" Click "Process All Files". This should just take a couple minutes Once processing is finished, cut and paste all object identity files (labeled with "Object Identities" in the file name) from your "A. Images" folder to your "C. Object Identities (Object Classification)" folder (within "G. Ilastik)

steps.title: Click "Output"
steps.instruction: Click "Output"

steps.title: Change the output folder to your "D
steps.instruction: Change the output folder to your "D. Object Identities Post-Color Change" folder. Using the "Copy File Path" shortcut makes this quite easy. Change "Output format:" to PNG

steps.title: Click "Open" (bottom left corner)
steps.instruction: Click "Open" (bottom left corner)

steps.title: Click "Open"
steps.instruction: Click "Open"

steps.title: Change the file path included in...
steps.instruction: Change the file path included in the macro to correspond with where the "ALL GREEN LUT.lut" file is located on your specific computer. Note: make sure to replace back slashes with forward slashes when you do this.

steps.title: In the Batch Process window, click "Process"
steps.instruction: In the Batch Process window, click "Process". Images (with some color now) should begin to show up in your "D. Object Identities Post Color-Change" folder. This usually takes around a minute

steps.title: Copy and paste all newly created files in "D
steps.instruction: Copy and paste all newly created files in "D. Object Identities Post-Color Change" to the "Segmentation" folder within "I. Nutil" Video

steps.title: Image Color-Change Macro
steps.instruction: Image Color-Change Macro

steps.title: Click "Save As" (top left)
steps.instruction: Click "Save As" (top left)

steps.title: Save the Nutil analysis file within your "I
steps.instruction: Save the Nutil analysis file within your "I. Nutil" folder, named according to the cohort, animal, and hemisphere (sagittal) you are working on

steps.title: Click "Start" (bottom left)
steps.instruction: Click "Start" (bottom left). This should take a couple minutes. All results will appear in your "Output" folder (within "I. Nutil"

steps.title: Run Nutil again for each hemisphere/condition...
steps.instruction: Run Nutil again for each hemisphere/condition you are analyzing, making sure to change relevant settings when switching between hemispheres/conditions. Note: ensure that you change all appropriate input/output folders. It is extremely easy to mix things up here

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: Sections 1 through 3 should be completed for each brain (coronal sections) or hemisphere (sagittal sections) being analyzed together before moving on to Sections 4 through 6.

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: The preceding steps only need to be completed the first time you use Quint. Each subsequent time, you can begin with Section 1 below.

steps.title: Open this link (mice) or this...
steps.instruction: Open this link (mice) or this link (rats) Click "Choose Files" Open your "I. Nutil" folder Navigate to and select "PROJECTNAME_3D_combined.json" within "Output" – "Coordinates" Note: there is no way to save a MeshView visualization. You will have to reload this file every time you want to visualize it. Change the opacity of the displayed atlas brain meshes using the sliders in the left panel. To obtain a translucent whole brain mesh that is good for visualization, drag the "All" slider all the way to the left and the "Root" slider about 20% of the way to the right Toggle the brain regions of PNNs displayed using the sliders in the right panel

steps.title: Use the following sections for general...
steps.instruction: Use the following sections for general reference throughout the mapping process: Mapping Landmarks (Coronal) Mapping Landmarks (Sagittal) Overall Mapping Strategy

steps.title: Use the following sections for specific...
steps.instruction: Use the following sections for specific steps to follow while mapping: Opening and Configuring QuickNII Mapping Mechanics (Coronal) Mapping Mechanics (Sagittal) Finishing Up Mapping

steps.title: Refer back to general reference sections as needed for clarification
steps.instruction: Refer back to general reference sections as needed for clarification

steps.title: Check your work (with another person...
steps.instruction: Check your work (with another person if at all possible) before moving on to VisuAlign. After QuickNII, images should be mostly mapped, with all structures being roughly the same size when you compare images to the atlas. Slight deviation in alignment of regions between images and the atlas is acceptable in QuickNII and correctable later with VisuAlign.

description: background (dark green/black) (Only Labels 1 and 2 are visible below) To annotate: select a label in the left panel and draw on the image, marking a few of that label's pixels To erase an annotation: select eraser (below "Add Label" button on left panel); draw to erase To navigate the image: hold "Ctrl" key and hover your mouse over the area you want to zoom in on; scroll to zoom in and out Viewing segmentation results: Select "Live Update" (left panel) Toggle "Prediction" and "Segmentation" eyeball icons for each label as needed to check segmentation Note: "Segmentation" views are generally most useful
